prompts.intro: You are going to visit your cousin next weekend. Write an email to your cousin. In your email:
bullets: say when you are going to arrive
say what you want to do together
ask one question about the weekend

prompts.intro: Your friend helped you move to a new flat last Saturday. Write an email to your friend. In your email:
bullets: thank your friend for helping you
say something you like about your new flat
invite your friend to visit you soon

prompts.intro: You cannot go to your friend's birthday party next Friday. Write an email to your friend. In your email:
bullets: say why you cannot go to the party
say something nice about your friend's birthday
suggest another day to celebrate together

prompts.intro: You want to invite a classmate to study with you at the library. Write an email to your classmate. In your email:
bullets: say which day and time you want to meet
explain why you need help with your studies
ask if your classmate is free that day

prompts.intro: You went on a school trip to a science museum yesterday. Write an email to your parent. In your email:
bullets: say what you saw at the museum
say what you enjoyed most about the trip
ask a question about the weekend

prompts.intro: You found a package outside your neighbour's door. Write a note to your neighbour. In your note:
bullets: say where you found the package
say what you did with it
ask your neighbour to contact you

prompts.intro: You have just started a new hobby. Write an email to a friend. In your email:
bullets: say what your new hobby is
explain why you like it
ask if your friend wants to try it with you

prompts.intro: You received an invitation to your classmate's house for dinner next week. Write an email to your classmate. In your email:
bullets: say you are happy to come to dinner
ask what time you should arrive
offer to bring something for dinner

prompts.intro: You and a classmate are working on a school project together. Write an email to your classmate. In your email:
bullets: say what part of the project you have finished
suggest a time to meet and work together
ask your classmate a question about the project

prompts.intro: You are going on holiday with your family next month. Write an email to a friend. In your email:
bullets: say where you are going on holiday
say what you are planning to do there
ask your friend about their summer plans

prompts.intro: You saw a film last weekend that you really liked. Write an email to a friend. In your email:
bullets: say what film you saw
say why you liked the film
suggest watching it together

prompts.intro: You are going to take part in a school sports event next week. Write an email to your parent. In your email:
bullets: say what sport you are going to do
say when and where the event is
ask your parent to come and watch you

prompts.intro: Your neighbour's dog came into your garden yesterday. Write a note to your neighbour. In your note:
bullets: say what happened with the dog
say what you did to help the dog
ask your neighbour to check their garden fence

prompts.intro: You want to ask a friend for help with your English homework. Write an email to your friend. In your email:
bullets: explain what homework you need help with
say when you need to finish it
ask if you can meet at your friend's house

prompts.intro: You have just moved to a new school. Write an email to your old classmate. In your email:
bullets: describe something about your new school
say how you feel about the change
ask about what is happening at your old school

prompts.intro: You want to visit a new café in town with a friend. Write an email to your friend. In your email:
bullets: say how you heard about the café
say why you think it sounds nice
suggest a day and time to go

prompts.intro: You missed an important class last week because you were ill. Write an email to your classmate. In your email:
bullets: explain why you missed the class
ask what the teacher talked about in class
ask if you can borrow your classmate's notes

prompts.intro: You are going to take a cooking class next month. Write an email to a family member. In your email:
bullets: say what kind of cooking class it is
explain why you want to learn to cook
ask the family member for a favourite recipe

prompts.intro: You played in a football match last Saturday. Write an email to a friend. In your email:
bullets: say what the result of the match was
describe one thing that happened during the game
invite your friend to come and watch your next match

prompts.intro: You want to buy a birthday present for a classmate but you need some ideas. Write an email to another classmate. In your email:
bullets: say whose birthday it is and when
ask for ideas about what to buy
suggest going to the shops together

prompts.intro: You are thinking about joining a local sports club. Write an email to a friend who is already a member. In your email:
bullets: say which sport you are interested in
ask about the days and times of the classes
ask if you can go with your friend one day to try it

prompts.intro: You had a great family dinner last Sunday for your grandparent's birthday. Write an email to a cousin who could not come. In your email:
bullets: say what food you ate at the dinner
describe something funny or special that happened
ask why your cousin could not come

prompts.intro: You are going to a concert with a friend next weekend. Write an email to your friend. In your email:
bullets: say what time you want to meet before the concert
suggest a place to have dinner before the show
ask your friend what they want to wear

prompts.intro: You borrowed a book from a classmate last month and you want to return it. Write an email to your classmate. In your email:
bullets: say something you liked about the book
explain when you can return the book
ask your classmate if they have another book you can borrow

prompts.intro: You are going to visit a new city for the first time next month. Write an email to a friend who lives there. In your email:
bullets: say when you are going to arrive in the city
ask your friend to recommend some places to visit
suggest meeting up while you are there

prompts.intro: Your class is going to have a picnic in the park next Friday. Write an email to a classmate. In your email:
bullets: say what food you are going to bring to the picnic
ask your classmate what they are bringing
suggest a game you can all play in the park

prompts.intro: You found a lost cat near your house yesterday. Write a note to your neighbour. In your note:
bullets: describe what the cat looks like
say where you found the cat
ask your neighbour if they know who the cat belongs to

prompts.intro: You want to start a study group with some classmates. Write an email to a classmate. In your email:
bullets: explain why you think a study group is a good idea
say which subject you want to study first
ask your classmate if they want to join the group

prompts.intro: You are going to start learning to play a musical instrument. Write an email to a family member. In your email:
bullets: say which instrument you are going to learn
explain why you chose that instrument
ask the family member if they have any advice for you

prompts.intro: You are organising a surprise party for a friend next Saturday. Write an email to another friend. In your email:
bullets: say what time and where the party is going to be
ask the friend to help you with the preparations
say what you need the friend to bring to the party
